title: Establish reviewer rotation across all three review gates
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual's quality pipeline depends on three reviewer gates (clinical, advocate, translation). Single-person dependence on any gate is a sustainability risk as the glossary scales to more languages and terms in M4. Reviewer rotation across all three gates ensures continuity and removes bottlenecks.
objective: Establish a documented reviewer rotation policy and schedule across all three review gates (clinical, advocate, translation), ensuring no single reviewer is a single point of failure and covering upcoming review work as the glossary scales.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/reviewer-rotation.md is created defining: minimum reviewer bench per gate (at least 2 per gate recommended), rotation schedule, onboarding process for new reviewers (including qualification check per reviewers-001, COI, checklist familiarization), and escalation path if a reviewer is unavailable
Rotation plan covers all three gates: clinical, patient-advocate, and translation (per language pair)
Policy references the reviewer-qualification rules (reviewers-001) and COI requirements
At least the existing reviewers from reviewers-002 and translators-001 are listed as initial rotation members
output: docs/reviewer-rotation.md with the rotation policy, schedule, and onboarding process
